objective: Assessed the T03 plan for the bounded inner explorer agent loop (run_explore) with mode steering and harness-enforced tool gating against all 8 acceptance criteria and the actual T01/T02 dependency surfaces.
key_changes: run_explore signature matches AC-1 exactly; depends on the real ChatClient trait, ExploreConfig, and ExploreAnswer/ExploreError return types.
Verified Message/ChatRequest/ChatResponse/Tool/ToolCall APIs the loop uses all exist in client.rs with the exact methods invoked.
ClientError::Connection exists and maps cleanly to ExploreError::ProviderDown (AC-6).
Mode {Standard,Balanced,Aggressive} and allowed_tools present in config.rs; drive all gating.
All 7 ops return #[derive(Serialize)] types, so direct in-crate call + JSON serialization is feasible; lib name grove_core confirmed.
No new Cargo deps required (serde_json present, std::process::Command for shell).
Security sound: shell dispatch uses args vector, allowlist checked before spawn, no shell interpolation (AC-3).
ADVISORY: per-op argument parsing (heterogeneous op signatures + per-tool JSON schema) is under-specified and is the main real work item.
ADVISORY: Aggressive tool_choice hint contradicts AC-4 prompt-only steering and the aggressive_toolset_same_as_standard test; recommend dropping it.
ADVISORY: BALANCED_RECON_TURNS comment says 'from cfg' but no such config field exists; it is a module constant.
ADVISORY: build_full_toolset takes an apparently-unused root param; drop if unneeded to stay clippy-clean (AC-8).
10-test matrix covers all AC-7 scenarios; verdict Approved with advisory notes only.
